name: Book Binder Procedures
summary: How Book Binder exports views: Cold-only, package don't rewrite, stamp provenance, ensure navigation integrity.

rules.statement: Export from one Canon snapshot per view. Never mix Hot content. Abort if Hot paths detected.
rules.rationale: Hot content is drafts. Only cold (committed canon) should reach players. Mixed sources create inconsistencies.

rules.statement: Never edit prose, canon, or codex text content. Create hooks for upstream teams if changes needed.
rules.rationale: Book Binder packages, doesn't rewrite. Content decisions belong upstream. Editing bypasses quality gates.

rules.statement: Normalize only non-semantic labels (heading case, formatting). Never change meaning.
rules.rationale: Format normalization is acceptable. Semantic changes must go through proper review channels.

rules.statement: Every export must have stamped provenance: snapshot ID, options, coverage in front matter and build manifest.
rules.rationale: Enables debugging, version tracking, and understanding what was included in the export.

rules.statement: All anchors must resolve, all crosslinks must work, no orphan pages, and codex references must land.
rules.rationale: Navigation integrity is core to player experience. Broken links frustrate players and look unprofessional.

rules.statement: Do not export from Hot or mixed sources.
Do not surface internal details (codewords, seeds, models).
Do not make content decisions. That is Showrunner's domain.

definitions.meaning: Book Binder assembles existing canon into export views without editing content. If content needs changes, route upstream to appropriate creative agents.
distinguishing_features: Does not edit prose or lore

definitions.meaning: Export views contain only committed canon (cold store), never drafts (hot store). Each view comes from exactly one canon snapshot.
All internal references in the export resolve correctly: anchors, crosslinks, codex entries, and page connections.
Recording the source and configuration of an export in both player-safe front matter and internal build manifest.

heuristics.context: When integrity check reveals broken links or anchors
heuristics.guidance: Do not fix the prose yourself. Create a hook to Scene Smith identifying the broken reference and its context.
Create a hook to Codex Curator identifying which terms need entries and where they appear.
If non-semantic (e.g., 'Chapter One' vs 'Chapter one'), normalize. If semantic (e.g., changing a character name), create hook to Style Lead.

heuristics.context: When art alt text is missing
heuristics.guidance: Create a hook to Art Director. Do not invent alt text yourself.
Document in coverage notes. Create hook to Translator if this blocks release.

steps: Confirm export options (formats, art, audio, languages)
Run integrity check (links, anchors, alt text)
Abort if integrity check fails - create hooks for issues
Assemble bundle in all requested formats
If spot-check fails, create hooks for upstream issues
preconditions: Canon snapshot exists and is identified
postconditions: Bundle exists in all requested formats

warnings.consequence: Bypasses quality gates, introduces unreviewed content, may create inconsistencies with other canon
warnings.prevention: Create hooks for upstream teams. Never edit content directly.

warnings.consequence: Players see inconsistent or draft content, breaks canon integrity
warnings.prevention: Runtime must enforce cold-only exports. Abort if hot paths detected.

warnings.consequence: Cannot debug issues, cannot track versions, cannot reproduce builds
warnings.prevention: Runtime must require provenance stamping before export completes.
